{{Quote|They had it all. Orbital and ground-based cannons, hunter-killer droids, everything.|Jessika Pava, on Ikkrukk's defenses|<ref name="Poe 30" />}}
The '''hunter-killer droid''' was a type of [[security droid]] utilized by the [[Ikkrukk's government|government]] of the [[Mid Rim Territories|Mid Rim]] [[planet]] [[Ikkrukk]]. During the [[New Republic Era]], hunter-killer [[droid]]s were used in conjunction with [[orbit]]al and ground-based [[cannon]]s to form Ikkrukk's defenses. They, along with the rest of Ikkrukk's defensive systems, were disabled by [[Ikkrukkian]] [[Ikkrukk First Order sympathizers|sympathizers]] to the [[First Order]]<ref name="Poe 30" /> in [[34 ABY]].<ref name="Date">''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the [[Battle of Ikkrukk|events]] on [[Ikkrukk]], as depicted in [[Poe Dameron 30|''Poe Dameron'' 30]], to [[34 ABY]].</ref> This allowed the First Order to [[Battle of Ikkrukk|attack]] Ikkrukk during their [[First Order-Resistance War|conquest]] of [[the galaxy]].<ref name="Poe 30" />

At the same [[time]], [[General]] [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]] of the [[Resistance]] dispatched [[Black Squadron (Resistance)|Black Squadron]] to Ikkrukk to secure an alliance with the Ikkrukkians. The [[starfighter]] [[Squadron|group]] arrived in the midst of the First Order's attack, and the Black Squadron [[pilot]] [[Jessika Pava]] remarked that the planet's heavy defenses, including the hunter-killer droids, might have been the reason Organa wished to seek an alliance with Ikkrukk in the first place.<ref name="Poe 30" />

==Behind the scenes==
The hunter-killer droid model was first mentioned in [[Poe Dameron 30|''Poe Dameron'' 30]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[Charles Soule]] and published by [[Marvel Comics]]<ref name="Poe 30" /> on [[August 15]], [[2018]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/69617/star_wars_poe_dameron_2016_30|text=Star Wars: Poe Dameron (2016) #30}}</ref> Various droids with the "hunter-killer" designation previously appeared in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends continuity]],<ref name="DF">[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]]</ref><ref name="SGtD">''[[Scavenger's Guide to Droids]]''</ref><ref name="Stark">[[Star Wars (1998) 37|''Star Wars'' (1998) 37]]</ref><ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> beginning with the [[Hunter-Killer probot]] in the [[1992]] comic [[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]].<ref name="DE5">[[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]]</ref>
